plain_language.what_this_is: Objective, threshold-based signs of project bloat, grounded in real-world software research: large source files and too many dependencies. Deliberately narrow -- it flags size/count facts, it does NOT judge whether the growth is bad (that's your call).
plain_language.why_these: Web research on code/project bloat points to large files, duplicated code, dead code, and dependency bloat as the objective signals. Duplication and dead code need language-specific tooling and produce false alarms, so this starter measures only the two we can read cleanly and honestly here: file size and dependency count.
plain_language.adjustable: Raise/lower the numbers as the project grows; add source globs as new code areas appear.
